   524→def _detect_state_machine_transform(
   525→    input_rows: List[Dict[str, Any]],
   526→    output_rows: List[Dict[str, Any]],
   527→    kept_indices: List[int],
   528→    output_col: str,
   529→) -> Optional[StateMachineTransform]:
   530→    """Detect finite state machine for sequence labeling (segment IDs, phases)."""
   531→    if not input_rows or not output_rows:
   532→        return None
   533→
   534→    out_vals = [output_rows[i].get(output_col) for i in range(len(output_rows))]
   535→
   536→    # Output must be integer/string states
   537→    try:
   538→        out_ints = [int(v) for v in out_vals if v is not None]
   539→        if len(out_ints) < 2:
   540→            return None
   541→    except (ValueError, TypeError):
   542→        return None
   543→
   544→    # Look for pattern where state changes based on threshold/predicate in input column
   545→    for in_col in input_rows[0].keys():
   546→        # Try to find if state increments when input crosses threshold
   547→        in_vals_numeric = []
   548→        for i in kept_indices:
   549→            try:
   550→                val = float(input_rows[i].get(in_col, 0))
   551→                in_vals_numeric.append(val)
   552→            except (ValueError, TypeError):
   553→                in_vals_numeric.append(None)
   554→
   555→        if len(in_vals_numeric) < 2:
   556→            continue
   557→
   558→        # Build transition model: state[i] depends on state[i-1] and in_col[i]
   559→        # Check for increment on threshold cross
   560→        states = sorted(set(out_ints))
   561→        if len(states) > 16:
   562→            continue
   563→
   564→        # Detect threshold-based transitions
   565→        # Common pattern: stay in same state while condition is true, increment when it flips
   566→        # For simplicity, detect if segment_id increments when value crosses threshold
   567→
   568→        # Analyze transitions
   569→        transitions = []
   570→        thresholds = set()
   571→
   572→        for i in range(1, len(out_ints)):
   573→            prev_state = out_ints[i-1]
   574→            curr_state = out_ints[i]
   575→            in_val = in_vals_numeric[i]
   576→
   577→            if in_val is not None and curr_state != prev_state:
   578→                transitions.append({
   579→                    "trigger": "threshold",
   580→                    "input_value": in_val,
   581→                    "from_state": prev_state,
   582→                    "to_state": curr_state,
   583→                })
   584→
   585→        if len(transitions) > 0:
   586→            # Determine threshold from transitions
   587→            # All transitions should be of same "type"
   588→            # For segment_id pattern: new segment when value crosses threshold
   589→
   590→            # Check if all state increments are by 1
   591→            all_increments_by_1 = True
   592→            for i in range(1, len(out_ints)):
   593→                if out_ints[i] - out_ints[i-1] != 0 and out_ints[i] - out_ints[i-1] != 1:
   594→                    all_increments_by_1 = False
   595→                    break
   596→
   597→            if all_increments_by_1:
   598→                # Find threshold: value above which starts new segment
   599→                # Look for the value where state changes
   600→                threshold_values = [
   601→                    t["input_value"] for t in transitions
   602→                    if t["to_state"] - t["from_state"] == 1
   603→                ]
   604→
   605→                if len(threshold_values) > 0:
   606→                    # Use average or first threshold value
   607→                    threshold = sum(threshold_values) / len(threshold_values)
   608→
   609→                    return StateMachineTransform(
   610→                        input_column=in_col,
   611→                        output_column=output_col,
   612→                        states=[str(s) for s in states],
   613→                        transitions=[{
   614→                            "type": "threshold",
   615→                            "threshold": threshold,
   616→                            "increment": True,
   617→                        }],
   618→                        initial_state=str(states[0]),
   619→                        params={"mode": "segment_id"},
   620→                    )
   621→
   622→    return None

   625→def _detect_neighbor_filter(
   626→    input_rows: List[Dict[str, Any]],
   627→    output_rows: List[Dict[str, Any]],
   628→    kept_indices: List[int],
   629→    output_col: str = None,
   630→) -> Optional[NeighborFilterTransform]:
   631→    """Detect neighbor-based filtering (lookahead/lookbehind patterns)."""
   632→    if not input_rows or not output_rows:
   633→        return None
   634→
   635→    all_indices = set(range(len(input_rows)))
   636→    dropped = all_indices - set(kept_indices)
   637→
   638→    if not dropped:
   639→        return None
   640→
   641→    # Analyze patterns of dropped rows relative to neighbors
   642→    # Check if dropping depends on next/prev row values
   643→
   644→    for col in input_rows[0].keys():
   645→        dropped_by_next = True
   646→        dropped_by_prev = True
   647→
   648→        for idx in dropped:
   649→            if idx < len(input_rows) - 1:
   650→                next_val = input_rows[idx + 1].get(col)
   651→                if next_val is None:
   652→                    dropped_by_next = False
   653→                    break
   654→
   655→            if idx > 0:
   656→                prev_val = input_rows[idx - 1].get(col)
   657→                if prev_val is None:
   658→                    dropped_by_prev = False
   659→                    break
   660→
   661→        # Check for drop when next row has certain value
   662→        dropped_next_values = {}
   663→        for idx in dropped:
   664→            if idx < len(input_rows) - 1:
   665→                next_val = input_rows[idx + 1].get(col)
   666→                if next_val:
   667→                    key = str(next_val)
   668→                    dropped_next_values[key] = dropped_next_values.get(key, 0) + 1
   669→
   670→        # A column where most dropped rows have same "next" value
   671→        if dropped_next_values:
   672→            most_common_next = max(dropped_next_values.items(), key=lambda x: x[1])
   673→            if most_common_next[1] >= len(dropped) * 0.5:
   674→                return NeighborFilterTransform(
   675→                    condition=f"next.{col} == {repr(most_common_next[0])}",
   676→                    direction="next",
   677→                    params={"column": col, "value": most_common_next[0]},
   678→                )
   679→
   680→        # Check for drop when next row status == 'duplicate'
   681→        if col.lower() in ["status", "type", "flag"]:
   682→            dropped_next_status = {}
   683→            for idx in dropped:
   684→                if idx < len(input_rows) - 1:
   685→                    next_val = input_rows[idx + 1].get(col)
   686→                    if next_val:
   687→                        key = str(next_val)
   688→                        dropped_next_status[key] = dropped_next_status.get(key, 0) + 1
   689→
   690→            for status_val, count in dropped_next_status.items():
   691→                if count >= len(dropped) * 0.5:
   692→                    return NeighborFilterTransform(
   693→                        condition=f"next.{col} == {repr(status_val)}",
   694→                        direction="next",
   695→                        params={"column": col, "value": status_val, "pattern": "duplicate_next"},
   696→                    )
   697→
   698→    return None

   787→def _render_filter_body(filter_pred: Optional[FilterPredicate]) -> str:
   788→    if filter_pred is None:
   789→        return "    return True"
   790→    condition = filter_pred.condition
   791→    if "==" in condition:
   792→        parts = condition.split("==", 1)
   793→        col_name = parts[0].strip().strip("'\"")
   794→        right = parts[1].strip()
   795→        return f"    return row.get('{col_name}', '') == {right}" if right.isdigit() else f"    return str(row.get('{col_name}', '')) == {right}"
   796→    if "!=" in condition:
   797→        parts = condition.split("!=", 1)
   798→        col_name = parts[0].strip().strip("'\"")
   799→        right = parts[1].strip()
   800→        return f"    return row.get('{col_name}', '') != {right}" if right.isdigit() else f"    return str(row.get('{col_name}', '')) != {right}"
   801→    if "row_index in" in condition:
   802→        match = re.search(r"row_index in \[(.*)\]", condition)
   803→        if match:
   804→            return f"    return {match.group(1)}.__contains__(row_index)"
   805→    return "    return True"

   159→            in_row = input_rows[input_idx]
   160→            match = all(
   161→                str(out_row.get(k)) == str(in_row.get(k)) if in_row.get(k) is not None
   162→                else out_row.get(k) is None
   163→                for k in out_row
   164→            )
   165→            if match:
   166→                output_indices.append(input_idx)
   167→                input_idx += 1
   168→                break
   169→            input_idx += 1
   170→
   171→    output_columns = set(output_rows[0].keys()) if output_rows else set()
   172→    input_columns = set(input_rows[0].keys()) if input_rows else set()
   173→
   174→    kept_indices = set(output_indices)
   175→    filter_pred = _build_filter_predicate(input_rows, kept_indices)
   176→
   177→    column_transforms: Dict[str, ColumnTransform] = {}
   178→    prefix_transforms: List[PrefixTransform] = []
   179→    window_transforms: List[WindowTransform] = []
   180→    state_machine_transforms: List[StateMachineTransform] = []
   181→    neighbor_filter: Optional[NeighborFilter] = None
   182→
   183→    for out_col in output_columns:
   184→        possible_input_cols = [c for c in input_columns
   185→                              if c.lower() == out_col.lower() or c == out_col]
   186→
   187→        if not possible_input_cols:
   188→            col_transform = _infer_computed_column(out_col, input_rows, output_rows, output_indices)
   189→            if col_transform:
   190→                column_transforms[out_col] = col_transform
   191→                continue
   192→            values = {row.get(out_col) for row in output_rows}
   193→            if len(values) == 1:
   194→                column_transforms[out_col] = ColumnTransform(
   195→                    input_column=None,
   196→                    transform_type="const",
   197→                    params={"value": list(values)[0]},
   198→                )
   199→            else:
   200→                column_transforms[out_col] = ColumnTransform(
   201→                    input_column=None,
   202→                    transform_type="drop",
   203→                    params={},
   204→                )
   205→        else:
   206→            in_col = possible_input_cols[0]
   207→            col_transform = _infer_column_transform(in_col, out_col, input_rows, output_rows, output_indices)
   208→            column_transforms[out_col] = col_transform
   209→
   210→    for in_col in input_columns - output_columns:
   211→        if in_col not in {ct.input_column for ct in column_transforms.values() if ct.input_column}:
   212→            column_transforms[in_col] = ColumnTransform(
   213→                input_column=in_col,
   214→                transform_type="drop",
   215→                params={},
   216→            )
   217→
   218→    # Detect stateful transforms
   219→    prefix_transforms, window_transforms, state_machine_transforms, neighbor_filter = \
   220→        detect_stateful_transforms(input_rows, output_rows, list(kept_indices))
   221→
   222→    return column_transforms, filter_pred, prefix_transforms, window_transforms, state_machine_transforms, neighbor_filter
   223→
   224→
   225→def _build_filter_predicate(input_rows: List[Dict[str, Any]], kept_indices: set) -> Optional[FilterPredicate]:
   226→    if kept_indices == set(range(len(input_rows))):
   227→        return None
   228→
   229→    for row_idx in kept_indices:
   230→        if row_idx >= len(input_rows):
   231→            continue
   232→        row = input_rows[row_idx]
   233→        for col, val in row.items():
   234→            kept_values = [input_rows[i].get(col) for i in kept_indices]
   235→            dropped_values = [input_rows[i].get(col) for i in range(len(input_rows)) if i not in kept_indices]
   236→            if kept_values and dropped_values:
   237→                if all(v == val for v in kept_values) and not any(v == val for v in dropped_values):
   238→                    return FilterPredicate(f"{col} == {repr(val)}")
   239→                if all(v != val for v in kept_values) and any(v == val for v in dropped_values):
   240→                    return FilterPredicate(f"{col} != {repr(val)}")
   241→
   242→    return FilterPredicate(f"row_index in {sorted(kept_indices)}")
   243→
   244→
   245→def _infer_column_transform(
   246→    in_col: str,
   247→    out_col: str,
   248→    input_rows: List[Dict[str, Any]],
   249→    output_rows: List[Dict[str, Any]],
   250→    kept_indices: List[int],
   251→) -> ColumnTransform:
   252→    in_values = [input_rows[i].get(in_col) for i in kept_indices]
   253→    out_values = [output_rows[i].get(out_col) for i in range(len(output_rows))] if output_rows else []
   254→
   255→    if in_values == out_values:
   256→        return ColumnTransform(
   257→            input_column=in_col,
   258→            transform_type="identity" if in_col == out_col else "copy",
   259→            params={},
   260→        )
   261→
   262→    numeric_transform = _infer_numeric_transform(in_values, out_values)
   263→    if numeric_transform:
   264→        a, b = numeric_transform
   265→        return ColumnTransform(
   266→            input_column=in_col,
   267→            transform_type="numeric",
   268→            params={"a": a, "b": b},
   269→        )
   270→
   271→    if all(isinstance(v, str) for v in in_values + out_values):
   272→        if all(v.lower() == out_v for v, out_v in zip(in_values, out_values)):
   273→            return ColumnTransform(input_column=in_col, transform_type="string_lower", params={})
   274→        if all(v.upper() == out_v for v, out_v in zip(in_values, out_values)):
   275→            return ColumnTransform(input_column=in_col, transform_type="string_upper", params={})
   276→        if all(v.strip() == out_v for v, out_v in zip(in_values, out_values)):
   277→            return ColumnTransform(input_column=in_col, transform_type="string_trim", params={})
   278→
   279→    return ColumnTransform(input_column=in_col, transform_type="identity", params={})
   280→
   281→
   282→def _infer_computed_column(
   283→    out_col: str,
   284→    input_rows: List[Dict[str, Any]],
   285→    output_rows: List[Dict[str, Any]],
   286→    kept_indices: List[int],
   287→) -> Optional[ColumnTransform]:
   288→    values = [output_rows[i].get(out_col) for i in range(len(output_rows))]
   289→    if len(set(values)) == 1:
   290→        return ColumnTransform(input_column=None, transform_type="const", params={"value": values[0]})
   291→
   292→    for in_col1 in input_rows[0].keys() if input_rows else []:
   293→        for in_col2 in input_rows[0].keys() if input_rows else []:
   294→            if in_col1 == in_col2:
   295→                continue
   296→            test_values = [
   297→                str(input_rows[i].get(in_col1, "")) + str(input_rows[i].get(in_col2, ""))
   298→                for i in kept_indices
   299→            ]
   300→            out_vals = [output_rows[i].get(out_col, "") for i in range(len(output_rows))]
   301→            if test_values == out_vals:
   302→                return ColumnTransform(
   303→                    input_column=None,
   304→                    transform_type="string_concat",
   305→                    params={"columns": [in_col1, in_col2]},
   306→                )
   307→    return None
   308→
   309→
   310→def _infer_numeric_transform(in_values: List[Any], out_values: List[Any]) -> Optional[Tuple[float, float]]:
   311→    pairs = []
   312→    for in_val, out_val in zip(in_values, out_values):
   313→        try:
   314→            in_num = float(in_val) if in_val is not None else None
   315→            out_num = float(out_val) if out_val is not None else None
   316→            if in_num is not None and out_num is not None:
   317→                pairs.append((in_num, out_num))
   318→        except (ValueError, TypeError):
   319→            continue
   320→
   321→    if len(pairs) < 2:
   322→        return None
   323→
   324→    if len(pairs) == 2:
   325→        x1, y1 = pairs[0]
   326→        x2, y2 = pairs[1]
   327→        if x1 == x2:
   328→            return None
   329→        return ((y2 - y1) / (x2 - x1), y1 - ((y2 - y1) / (x2 - x1)) * x1)
   330→
   331→    n = len(pairs)
   332→    sum_x = sum(x for x, y in pairs)
   333→    sum_y = sum(y for x, y in pairs)
   334→    sum_xy = sum(x * y for x, y in pairs)
   335→    sum_xx = sum(x * x for x, y in pairs)
   336→
   337→    denom = n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x
   338→    if denom == 0:
   339→        return None
   340→
   341→    a = (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / denom
   342→    b = (sum_y - a * sum_x) / n
   343→    return (a, b)
   344→

   346→# ==============================================================================
   347→# Stateful Transform Detection (Part 2)
   348→# ==============================================================================
   349→
   350→def detect_stateful_transforms(
   351→    input_rows: List[Dict[str, Any]],
   352→    output_rows: List[Dict[str, Any]],
   353→    kept_indices: List[int],
   354→) -> Tuple[List[PrefixTransform], List[WindowTransform], List[StateMachineTransform], Optional[NeighborFilter]]:
   355→    """Detect stateful transforms from input/output sample."""
   356→    if not input_rows or not output_rows:
   357→        return [], [], [], None
   358→
   359��    prefix_transforms = []
   360→    window_transforms = []
   361→    state_machine_transforms = []
   362→    neighbor_filter = None
   363→
   364→    output_columns = set(output_rows[0].keys()) if output_rows else set()
   365→    input_columns = set(input_rows[0].keys()) if input_rows else set()
   366→
   367→    for out_col in output_columns:
   368→        # Try prefix transforms first
   369→        prefix_tf = _detect_prefix_transform(input_rows, output_rows, kept_indices, out_col)
   370→        if prefix_tf:
   371→            prefix_transforms.append(prefix_tf)
   372→            continue
   373→
   374→        # Try window transforms
   375→        window_tf = _detect_window_transform(input_rows, output_rows, kept_indices, out_col)
   376→        if window_tf:
   377→            window_transforms.append(window_tf)
   378→            continue
   379→
   380→        # Try state machine
   381→        sm_tf = _detect_state_machine_transform(input_rows, output_rows, kept_indices, out_col)
   382→        if sm_tf:
   383→            state_machine_transforms.append(sm_tf)
   384→
   385→    # Detect neighbor-based filtering
   386→    neighbor_filter = _detect_neighbor_filter(input_rows, output_rows, kept_indices)
   387→
   388→    return prefix_transforms, window_transforms, state_machine_transforms, neighbor_filter
   389→
   390→
   391→def _detect_prefix_transform(
   392→    input_rows: List[Dict[str, Any]],
   393→    output_rows: List[Dict[str, Any]],
   394→    kept_indices: List[int],
   395→    output_col: str,
   396→) -> Optional[PrefixTransform]:
   397→    """Detect prefix/cumulative transforms like running sum, average."""
   398→    if not input_rows or not output_rows:
   399→        return None
   400→
   401→    for in_col in input_rows[0].keys():
   402→        try:
   403→            in_vals = [float(input_rows[i].get(in_col, 0)) for i in kept_indices]
   404→            out_vals = [float(output_rows[i].get(output_col, 0)) for i in range(len(output_rows))]
   405→        except (ValueError, TypeError):
   406→            continue
   407→
   408→        if len(in_vals) != len(out_vals):
   409→            continue
   410→
   411→        # Check for prefix sum: out[i] = sum(in[0..i])
   412→        prefix_sum = 0
   413→        sum_match = True
   414→        for i, (iv, ov) in enumerate(zip(in_vals, out_vals)):
   415→            prefix_sum += iv
   416→            if abs(prefix_sum - ov) > 0.001:
   417→                sum_match = False
   418→                break
   419→
   420→        if sum_match and len(in_vals) > 1:
   421→            return PrefixTransform(
   422→                input_column=in_col,
   423→                output_column=output_col,
   424→                transform_type="sum",
   425→                params={},
   426→            )
   427→
   428→        # Check for prefix average: out[i] = mean(in[0..i])
   429→        prefix_sum = 0
   430→        avg_match = True
   431→        for i, (iv, ov) in enumerate(zip(in_vals, out_vals)):
   432→            prefix_sum += iv
   433→            expected_avg = prefix_sum / (i + 1)
   434→            if abs(expected_avg - ov) > 0.001:
   435→                avg_match = False
   436→                break
   437→
   438→        if avg_match and len(in_vals) > 1:
   439→            return PrefixTransform(
   440→                input_column=in_col,
   441→                output_column=output_col,
   442→                transform_type="avg",
   443→                params={},
   444→            )
   445→
   446→    return None
   447→
   448→
   449→def _detect_window_transform(
   450→    input_rows: List[Dict[str, Any]],
   451→    output_rows: List[Dict[str, Any]],
   452→    kept_indices: List[int],
   453→    output_col: str,
   454→) -> Optional[WindowTransform]:
   455→    """Detect sliding window transforms (running average over last W rows)."""
   456→    if not input_rows or not output_rows:
   457→        return None
   458→
   459→    for in_col in input_rows[0].keys():
   460→        try:
   461→            in_vals = [float(input_rows[i].get(in_col, 0)) for i in kept_indices]
   462→            out_vals = [float(output_rows[i].get(output_col, 0)) for i in range(len(output_rows))]
   463→        except (ValueError, TypeError):
   464→            continue
   465→
   466→        if len(in_vals) != len(out_vals):
   467→            continue
   468→
   469→        # Try window sizes 1-64
   470→        for window_size in range(1, 65):
   471→            # Check for window average
   472→            avg_match = True
   473→            for i, ov in enumerate(out_vals):
   474→                if i < window_size:
   475→                    window_start = max(0, i)
   476→                else:
   477→                    window_start = i - window_size + 1
   478→                window_vals = in_vals[window_start:i+1]
   479→                window_sum = sum(window_vals)
   480→                expected_avg = window_sum / len(window_vals)
   481→                if abs(expected_avg - ov) > 0.001:
   482→                    avg_match = False
   483→                    break
   484→
   485→            if avg_match and window_size <= len(in_vals):
   486→                return WindowTransform(
   487→                    input_column=in_col,
   488→                    output_column=output_col,
   489→                    window_size=window_size,
   490→                    transform_type="avg",
   491→                    params={},
   492→                )
   493→
   494→    return None
   495→
   496→
   497→def _detect_state_machine_transform(
   498→    input_rows: List[Dict[str, Any]],
   499→    output_rows: List[Dict[str, Any]],
   500→    kept_indices: List[int],
   501→    output_col: str,
   502→) -> Optional[StateMachineTransform]:
   503→    """Detect finite state machine for sequence labeling (segment IDs)."""
   504→    if not input_rows or not output_rows:
   505→        return None
   506→
   507→    out_vals = [output_rows[i].get(output_col) for i in range(len(output_rows))]
   508→
   509→    # Output must be integer states
   510→    try:
   511→        out_ints = [int(v) for v in out_vals if v is not None]
   512→        if len(out_ints) < 2:
   513→            return None
   514→    except (ValueError, TypeError):
   515→        return None
   516→
   517→    # Look for pattern where state changes based on threshold in input column
   518→    for in_col in input_rows[0].keys():
   519→        in_vals_numeric = []
   520→        for i in kept_indices:
   521→            try:
   522→                val = float(input_rows[i].get(in_col, 0))
   523→                in_vals_numeric.append(val)
   524→            except (ValueError, TypeError):
   525→                in_vals_numeric.append(None)
   526→
   527→        if len(in_vals_numeric) < 2:
   528→            continue
   529→
   530→        # Find threshold: value where state increments
   531→        transitions = []
   532→        for i in range(1, len(out_ints)):
   533→            prev_state = out_ints[i-1]
   534→            curr_state = out_ints[i]
   535→            in_val = in_vals_numeric[i]
   536→
   537→            if in_val is not None and curr_state != prev_state:
   538→                transitions.append({
   539→                    "from_state": prev_state,
   540→                    "to_state": curr_state,
   541→                    "input_value": in_val,
   542→                })
   543→
   544→        if len(transitions) > 0:
   545→            # Check if all state increments are by 1
   546→            all_increments_by_1 = True
   547→            for i in range(1, len(out_ints)):
   548→                diff = out_ints[i] - out_ints[i-1]
   549→                if diff != 0 and diff != 1:
   550→                    all_increments_by_1 = False
   551→                    break
   552→
   553→            if all_increments_by_1:
   554→                # Find threshold from transitions
   555→                threshold_values = [t["input_value"] for t in transitions
   556→                                   if t["to_state"] - t["from_state"] == 1]
   557→
   558→                if threshold_values:
   559→                    threshold = sum(threshold_values) / len(threshold_values)
   560→
   561→                    return StateMachineTransform(
   562→                        input_column=in_col,
   563→                        output_column=output_col,
   564→                        threshold=threshold,
   565→                        initial_state=out_ints[0],
   566→                        params={"mode": "segment_id"},
   567→                    )
   568→
   569→    return None
   570→
   571→
   572→def _detect_neighbor_filter(
   573→    input_rows: List[Dict[str, Any]],
   574→    output_rows: List[Dict[str, Any]],
   575→    kept_indices: List[int],
   576→) -> Optional[NeighborFilter]:
   577→    """Detect neighbor-based filtering (drop if next row has certain value)."""
   578→    if not input_rows or not output_rows:
   579→        return None
   580→
   581→    all_indices = set(range(len(input_rows)))
   582→    dropped = all_indices - set(kept_indices)
   583→
   584→    if not dropped:
   585→        return None
   586→
   587→    # Check for pattern: drop row i if row i+1 has status == 'duplicate'
   588→    for col in input_rows[0].keys():
   589→        dropped_next_values = {}
   590→        for idx in dropped:
   591→            if idx < len(input_rows) - 1:
   592→                next_val = input_rows[idx + 1].get(col)
   593→                if next_val:
   594→                    key = str(next_val)
   595→                    dropped_next_values[key] = dropped_next_values.get(key, 0) + 1
   596→
   597→        if dropped_next_values:
   598→            most_common_next = max(dropped_next_values.items(), key=lambda x: x[1])
   599→            if most_common_next[1] >= len(dropped) * 0.5:
   600→                return NeighborFilter(
   601→                    column=col,
   602→                    value=most_common_next[0],
   603→                    pattern="duplicate_next",
   604→                )
   605→
   606→    return None
   607→

   609→# ==============================================================================
   610→# Code Generation - Base Template (Part 1)
   611→# ==============================================================================
   612→
   613→def _generate_transform_line(col: str, transform: ColumnTransform) -> str:
   614→    if transform.transform_type == "identity":
   615→        return f"    output_row['{col}'] = _infer_value_type(str(row.get('{col}', '')))"
   616→    if transform.transform_type == "copy":
   617→        return f"    output_row['{col}'] = _infer_value_type(str(row.get('{col}', '')))"
   618→    if transform.transform_type == "const":
   619→        val = transform.params.get("value", "")
   620→        if val is True:
   621→            val_str = "True"
   622→        elif val is False:
   623→            val_str = "False"
   624→        elif val is None:
   625→            val_str = "None"
   626→        elif isinstance(val, str):
   627→            val_str = f'"{val}"'
   628→        else:
   629→            val_str = str(val)
   630→        return f"    output_row['{col}'] = {val_str}"
   631→    if transform.transform_type == "numeric":
   632→        a = transform.params.get("a", 1)
   633→        b = transform.params.get("b", 0)
   634→        return f"    output_row['{col}'] = (float(row.get('{transform.input_column}', 0)) * {a}) + {b}"
   635→    if transform.transform_type == "string_lower":
   636→        return f"    output_row['{col}'] = str(row.get('{transform.input_column}', '')).lower()"
   637→    if transform.transform_type == "string_upper":
   638→        return f"    output_row['{col}'] = str(row.get('{transform.input_column}', '')).upper()"
   639→    if transform.transform_type == "string_trim":
   640→        return f"    output_row['{col}'] = str(row.get('{transform.input_column}', '')).strip()"
   641→    if transform.transform_type == "string_concat":
   642→        cols = transform.params.get("columns", [])
   643→        concat_expr = " + ".join(f"str(row.get('{c}', ''))" for c in cols)
   644→        return f"    output_row['{col}'] = {concat_expr}"
   645→    return f"    output_row['{col}'] = _infer_value_type(str(row.get('{col}', '')))"
   646→
   647→
   648→def _render_filter_body(filter_pred: Optional[FilterPredicate]) -> str:
   649→    if filter_pred is None:
   650→        return "    return True"
   651→    condition = filter_pred.condition
   652→    if "==" in condition:
   653→        parts = condition.split("==", 1)
   654→        col_name = parts[0].strip().strip("'\"")
   655→        right = parts[1].strip()
   656→        return f"    return row.get('{col_name}', '') == {right}" if right.isdigit() else f"    return str(row.get('{col_name}', '')) == {right}"
   657→    if "!=" in condition:
   658→        parts = condition.split("!=", 1)
   659→        col_name = parts[0].strip().strip("'\"")
   660→        right = parts[1].strip()
   661→        return f"    return row.get('{col_name}', '') != {right}" if right.isdigit() else f"    return str(row.get('{col_name}', '')) != {right}"
   662→    if "row_index in" in condition:
   663→        match = re.search(r"row_index in \[(.*)\]", condition)
   664→        if match:
   665→            return f"    return {match.group(1)}.__contains__(row_index)"
   666→    return "    return True"
   667→

   669→# ==============================================================================
   670→# Code Generation - Python Output
   671→# ==============================================================================
   672→
   673→def generate_python_code(
   674→    module_name: str,
   675→    data_type: DataType,
   676→    column_transforms: Dict[str, ColumnTransform],
   677→    filter_pred: Optional[FilterPredicate],
   678→    prefix_transforms: List[PrefixTransform],
   679→    window_transforms: List[WindowTransform],
   680→    state_machine_transforms: List[StateMachineTransform],
   681→    neighbor_filter: Optional[NeighborFilter],
   682→) -> str:
   683→    output_columns = [col for col, tr in column_transforms.items() if tr.transform_type != "drop"]
   684→    transform_lines = [_generate_transform_line(col, tr) for col, tr in column_transforms.items() if tr.transform_type != "drop"]
   685→    filter_code = _render_filter_body(filter_pred)
   686→    transform_lines_str = "\n".join(transform_lines)
   687→    output_columns_list = output_columns
   688→    data_type_val = data_type.value
   689→
